Leadership map

A point-in-time directory of officeholders (current as of 2026-07-04). 11188 records across 3 levels. Separate overlay — NOT part of the formally-verified funding graph. Every record carries a source. Roles churn; acting / former are marked. ← back to the map

By branch: legislative: 8000 · judicial: 1814 · law_enforcement: 1105 · state: 106 · executive: 103 · independent: 32 · military: 28

Jump to: Federal State County

Federal (2191)

Executive (103)
OfficePersonPartySinceStatusSource
Acting Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor)
Bureau of Labor Statistics
Career Deputy Commissioner serving as Acting Commissioner since early Aug 2025, after Trump fired confirmed Commissioner Erika McEntarfer on 2025-08-01. First replacement pick E.J. Antoni (nominated ~2025-08-11) was withdrawn ~Sept 2025.		William J. Wiatrowski2025-08-01actingsrc
Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor) — nominee
Bureau of Labor Statistics
Career BLS economist nominated by Trump in late Jan 2026 after the E.J. Antoni nomination was withdrawn. Senate HELP Committee vote set for 2026-06-24; no source confirms full Senate confirmation as of 2026-06-29.		Brett Matsumotonominatedsrc
Bureau of Land Management Director (Interior)
Bureau of Land Management
Former NM GOP congressman and state party chair; Senate-confirmed 46-43 on 2026-05-18, sworn in 2026-05-19 as the 20th BLM Director. Sometimes formally listed as 'Stevan Pearce.'		Steve PearceRepublican2026-05-19incumbentsrc
Acting Director, CDC (HHS)
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
Serving as Acting CDC Director concurrently with his role as NIH Director. Predecessor Susan Monarez (confirmed July 2025) was ousted 2025-08-27; Jim O'Neill acted Aug 2025-Feb 2026 before leaving for NSF.		Jay BhattacharyaRepublican2026-02-18actingsrc
Administrator, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS)
Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
17th CMS Administrator; Senate-confirmed 2025-04-03 (53-45), assumed office 2025-04-08.		Mehmet OzRepublican2025-04-08incumbentsrc
CISA Director, Acting (DHS)
Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
Executive Assistant Director for Cybersecurity serving as acting CISA Director since ~2026-02-26, after Madhu Gottumukkala was reassigned within DHS. Nominee Sean Plankey withdrew in April 2026; no Senate-confirmed director.		Nick AndersenRepublican2026-02-26actingsrc
Acting Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
Executive Office of the President
CEA Vice Chairman (Feb 2025) who became Acting Chair when Stephen Miran took leave for the Fed Board; remains acting chair pending Senate action on nominee Christopher Phelan.		Pierre Yared2025-09-16actingsrc
Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
Executive Office of the President
First Trump-2 CEA chair (confirmed mid-March 2025; start date approximate). Took leave Sept 2025 for an interim Federal Reserve Board seat, then formally resigned the CEA chair 2026-02-03 to remain at the Fed. Notable predecessor.		Stephen MiranRepublican2025-03-13 – 2026-02-03formersrc
Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (nominee)
Executive Office of the President
University of Minnesota economist nominated 2026-04-21 to chair CEA; Senate Banking Committee confirmation hearing held 2026-06-18. Not yet confirmed as of as_of date.		Christopher Phelannominatedsrc
Chair of the Council on Environmental Quality
Executive Office of the President
13th CEQ chair; acting from 2025-01-20, Senate-confirmed mid-September 2025.		Katherine ScarlettRepublican2025-09-18incumbentsrc
Counsel to the President (White House Counsel)
Executive Office of the President		David WarringtonRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Deputy National Security Advisor
Executive Office of the President
Named deputy NSA in the May 2025 NSC restructuring; concurrently serves as VP JD Vance's national security advisor (since Jan 2025). Included as a co-deputy NSA.		Andy BakerRepublican2025-05-01incumbentsrc
Deputy National Security Advisor
Executive Office of the President
Longtime Rubio aide promoted from State Dept (Counselor / Director of Policy Planning) to deputy NSA on 2026-05-26, described by WaPo/Axios as the 'top White House national security post.' Serves alongside other deputy NSAs Andy Baker and Robert Gabriel Jr.; no source uses the literal 'Principal Deputy' title.		Michael NeedhamRepublican2026-05-26incumbentsrc
Director of the Domestic Policy Council
Executive Office of the President
DPC Director since inauguration; still serving in 2026.		Vince HaleyRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Director of the National Economic Council
Executive Office of the President
Still NEC director as of mid-2026. Considered for Fed Chair but Trump nominated Kevin Warsh (Jan 2026) instead, explicitly to keep Hassett at the NEC.		Kevin HassettRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy (Drug Czar)
Executive Office of the President
Nominated March 2025; Senate-confirmed 52-48 on 2026-01-06 as the 10th ONDCP Director and first woman to lead the office.		Sara CarterRepublican2026-01-06incumbentsrc
Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
Executive Office of the President
Took over PPO on 2025-10-13 after Sergio Gor was confirmed Ambassador to India; concurrently remains a White House Deputy Chief of Staff (Assistant to the President) since 2025-01-20.		Dan ScavinoRepublican2025-10-13incumbentsrc
Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
Executive Office of the President
Led PPO from inauguration; nominated U.S. Ambassador to India (Aug 2025) and confirmed, ending PPO tenure (~Oct 2025); serving as Ambassador to India since 2026.		Sergio GorRepublican2025-01-20 – 2025-10-13formersrc
Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy
Executive Office of the President
Senate-confirmed OSTP director (74-25) on 2025-03-25; still serving in 2026.		Michael KratsiosRepublican2025-03-25incumbentsrc
Director of the White House Office of Public Liaison
Executive Office of the President
Deputy Assistant to the President and Director of the Office of Public Liaison since Jan 2025; still listed as current in 2026 (LegiStorm; Punchbowl 2026 Power List).		Jim GoyerRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Homeland Security Advisor
Executive Office of the President
Holds the Homeland Security Advisor title concurrently with White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy since inauguration (same person as wh-deputy-cos-policy).		Stephen MillerRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
National Security Advisor
Executive Office of the President
Original second-term NSA; removed 2025-05-01 in the first major shakeup (amid 'Signalgate') and subsequently moved toward the UN Ambassador post. Included as the notable predecessor.		Michael WaltzRepublican2025-01-20 – 2025-05-01formersrc
National Security Advisor (Acting)
Executive Office of the President
Assumed the role in an acting capacity 2025-05-01 after Mike Waltz was removed; concurrently remains Secretary of State (first dual-hat since Kissinger). Still acting NSA as of mid-2026 per NPR (2026-03-14); no permanent NSA named.		Marco RubioRepublican2025-05-01actingsrc
Senior Advisor to the President (de facto head of DOGE)
Executive Office of the President
Served as Senior Advisor and de facto DOGE head as a special government employee; departed late May 2025 when the 130-day SGE limit expired (sources vary May 28 vs May 30, 2025). Included as a notable former senior advisor.		Elon Musk2025-01-20 – 2025-05-30formersrc
Senior Counselor to the President for Trade and Manufacturing
Executive Office of the President
Assistant to the President and Senior Counselor for Trade and Manufacturing; in office since inauguration.		Peter NavarroRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House AI and Crypto Czar (Special Advisor for AI and Crypto)
Executive Office of the President
Left the czar role late March 2026 after exhausting his 130-day special-government-employee limit, moving to co-chair PCAST. No successor named; the AI and Crypto Czar position is vacant as of the as_of date.		David SacksRepublican2025-01-20 – 2026-03-26formersrc
White House Cabinet Secretary
Executive Office of the President
Held the dual title 'Deputy Chief of Staff for Communications and Personnel, and Cabinet Secretary' from inauguration; departed by 2025-10-01 (announced 2025-09-24, Axios). No clearly named successor as Cabinet Secretary was found and the related Director of Cabinet Affairs (Lea Bardon) also left ~April 2026, so the Cabinet Secretary role appears vacant/absorbed as of the as_of date — included only as the sourced former holder.		Taylor BudowichRepublican2025-01-20 – 2025-10-01formersrc
White House Communications Director
Executive Office of the President		Steven CheungRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs
Executive Office of the President
Took a leave of absence (~April 2026) to run Trump's 2026 midterm political operation; retains an Assistant to the President title while Richard Walters fills the role in an acting capacity. Some June 2026 sources still attribute the title to Blair, so 'former' reflects his stepping away from active duties rather than a clean departure.		James BlairRepublican2025-01-20 – 2026-04-10formersrc
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs (Acting)
Executive Office of the President
Former RNC chief of staff who joined the White House ~2026-05-01 to fill this role while James Blair is on leave running Trump's 2026 midterm operation; Wikipedia/LegiStorm describe him as acting.		Richard WaltersRepublican2026-05-01actingsrc
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Operations
Executive Office of the President
William 'Beau' Harrison; serving since Jan 2025 through mid-2026.		Beau HarrisonRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy
Executive Office of the President
Concurrently serves as Homeland Security Advisor (see wh-homeland-security-advisor).		Stephen MillerRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Strategic Implementation
Executive Office of the President
Still holds the role as of late June 2026, but reported (June 2026) to be slated to become VP JD Vance's chief of staff around August 2026 — record may go stale shortly after the as_of date.		Nick LunaRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House Press Secretary
Executive Office of the President		Karoline LeavittRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
White House Staff Secretary
Executive Office of the President		Will ScharfRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
FAA Administrator (DOT)
Federal Aviation Administration
Former Republic Airways CEO; Senate-confirmed 53-43 on 2025-07-09; FAA welcomed him as Administrator 2025-07-10.		Bryan BedfordRepublican2025-07-09incumbentsrc
FEMA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
Federal Emergency Management Agency
Career FEMA official (Region 9 Administrator since 2015) serving as acting head from ~May 2026 amid repeated 2025-2026 leadership turnover (Cameron Hamilton, David Richardson, et al.). Trump nominated Cameron Hamilton (~2026-05-11) as permanent Administrator. FEMA has had no Senate-confirmed administrator under the second Trump term.		Robert J. Fenton Jr.actingsrc
FinCEN Director (Treasury)
Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
Career/non-partisan official; assumed office September 2023, retained under the Trump administration. Still in role per HSGAC Senate letter dated 2026-04-17.		Andrea Gackiincumbentsrc
Acting Commissioner of Food and Drugs / FDA Commissioner (HHS)
Food and Drug Administration
Elevated from FDA Deputy Commissioner for Food to Acting Commissioner after Marty Makary resigned/was forced out ~2026-05-12. No permanent nominee confirmed as of 2026-06-29 (Heidi Overton reported as a leading candidate).		Kyle DiamantasRepublican2026-05-12actingsrc
IRS Commissioner (Treasury)
Internal Revenue Service
Office churned through several acting heads in 2025 (Gary Shapley, Michael Faulkender ~Apr 2025); Billy Long sworn in 2025-06-16 as 51st Commissioner, removed 2025-08-08, after which Treasury Secretary Bessent took over as acting. IRS.gov still lists him as 'Commissioner (Acting)' mid-2026; Senate Finance Democrats argue his statutory acting term expired 2026-03-06.		Scott BessentRepublican2025-08-08actingsrc
Acting Director, National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC)
National Counterterrorism Center (ODNI)
Elevated from NCTC Deputy Director ~2026-03-18 after Director Joe Kent (confirmed 2025-07-30) resigned 2026-03-17 over U.S. entry into the Iran war.		Joseph 'Joe' Weirsky2026-03-18actingsrc
NHTSA Administrator (DOT)
National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
Former NHTSA Chief Counsel (2017-2021); Senate-confirmed on 2025-09-18. Peter Simshauser had been Acting Administrator prior.		Jonathan MorrisonRepublican2025-09-18incumbentsrc
NIST Director / Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology (Commerce)
National Institute of Standards and Technology
Nominated Oct 2025; Senate-confirmed 46-45 on 2026-05-19. 18th NIST Director; former dean of engineering at Purdue University.		Arvind RamanRepublican2026-05-19incumbentsrc
Director, National Institutes of Health (HHS)
National Institutes of Health
18th NIH Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-25, took office 2025-04-01. Also serving as Acting CDC Director since 2026-02-18.		Jay BhattacharyaRepublican2025-04-01incumbentsrc
NNSA Administrator / Under Secretary for Nuclear Security (DOE)
National Nuclear Security Administration
Former U.S. Rep. (NY-22); Senate-confirmed 2025-09-18 in a party-line block vote. The Under Secretary for Nuclear Security also serves as NNSA Administrator.		Brandon WilliamsRepublican2025-09-18incumbentsrc
NOAA Administrator / Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere (Commerce)
National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
Nominated Feb 2025; Senate-confirmed early Oct 2025 (reported Oct 7-8, 2025). Previously acting NOAA administrator in first Trump term.		Neil JacobsRepublican2025-10-08incumbentsrc
Acting National Park Service Director (Interior)
National Park Service
Career NPS official (former comptroller); acting director since 2025-01-20. Trump nominee Scott Socha (nominated 2026-02-11) was withdrawn 2026-04-28, so no Senate-confirmed director is in place as of 2026-06-29.		Jessica Bowron2025-01-20actingsrc
Director, National Reconnaissance Office (NRO)
National Reconnaissance Office
Career SES; NRO director since 2019, serving as lame-duck. Roger Mason nominated to succeed (SSCI hearing 2026-05-28; SASC hearing 2026-06-25); not yet Senate-confirmed as of as_of date.		Dr. Christopher J. Scoleseincumbentsrc
Comptroller of the Currency (Treasury)
Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
Sworn in as the 32nd Comptroller of the Currency on 2025-07-15.		Jonathan V. GouldRepublican2025-07-15incumbentsrc
Acting Director of National Intelligence (DNI)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence
CHANGE since early 2025: Tulsi Gabbard (confirmed DNI 2025-02) announced resignation 2026-05-22 (effective 2026-06-19). Trump named Pulte acting DNI on 2026-06-02 (took over 2026-06-19), bypassing statutory successor PDDNI Aaron Lukas; Pulte concurrently remains FHFA Director with no prior intelligence background. Jay Clayton (SDNY U.S. Attorney) was nominated as permanent DNI 2026-06-11, pending/unconfirmed.		William J. 'Bill' PulteRepublican2026-06-19actingsrc
Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence (PDDNI)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence
Career intelligence officer. Senate-confirmed 2025-07-22 (51-46), sworn 2025-07-24. Briefly designated acting DNI on 2026-05-22 but Pulte was installed instead; Lukas continues as confirmed #2.		Aaron Lukas2025-07-24incumbentsrc
Acting Surgeon General of the United States (HHS)
Office of the Surgeon General, U.S. Public Health Service
OSG senior adviser carrying out Surgeon General duties on an interim basis (announced ~2026-05-19/20). Pending Senate nominee is Nicole Saphier (nominated 2026-04-30). Prior nominees withdrawn: Casey Means (2026-04-30) and Janette Nesheiwat (2025).		Stephanie HaridopolosRepublican2026-05-19actingsrc
USAID Administrator (Acting)
U.S. Agency for International Development (functions merged into U.S. Department of State)
USAID was dismantled in 2025: Secretary of State Rubio was designated Acting Administrator in early Feb 2025 and oversaw the wind-down. USAID officially ceased implementing foreign assistance 2025-07-01, with remaining functions absorbed into the State Department (NPR, 2025-07-01). No Senate-confirmed USAID Administrator was installed.		Marco RubioRepublicanformersrc
Secretary of the Air Force
U.S. Air Force
27th Secretary of the Air Force (also oversees the Space Force); former Principal Deputy Director of the NRO. Start date approximate (confirmed/sworn May 2025).		Troy MeinkRepublican2025-05-13incumbentsrc
Secretary of the Army
U.S. Army
26th Secretary of the Army; confirmed and sworn in 2025-02-25. Reported clashes with SecDef Hegseth (Washington Post, 2026-04) but remains in office as of June 2026.		Daniel DriscollRepublican2025-02-25incumbentsrc
Performing the Duties of the Director, U.S. Census Bureau (Commerce)
U.S. Census Bureau
Title is 'Performing the Duties of Director' (acting); no Senate-confirmed director. Announced via Sept 19, 2025 memo, taking over from acting director Ron Jarmin (who followed the Feb 2025 departure of director Robert Santos).		George M. CookRepublican2025-09-19actingsrc
Deputy Secretary of Defense
U.S. Department of Defense
Cerberus co-founder; confirmed 59-40 on 2025-03-14, sworn in 2025-03-17 as 36th Deputy Secretary of Defense.		Stephen FeinbergRepublican2025-03-17incumbentsrc
Under Secretary of Education (also performing delegated duties of Deputy Secretary)
U.S. Department of Education
No Senate-confirmed Deputy Secretary of Education (Penny Schwinn's nomination withdrawn 2025-09-03). Kent confirmed as Under Secretary 50-45 on 2025-08-01, sworn in 2025-08-04, holds delegated authority to perform Deputy Secretary functions. A March 2025 EO targeted the Department for dismantlement, but Congress has not abolished it.		Nicholas KentRepublican2025-08-04incumbentsrc
Acting Director, Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence (DOE)
U.S. Department of Energy, Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence
Career DOE-IN official, acting since ~early 2026 after Jay Tilden departed (position not Senate-confirmed). Exact acting-start date is a soft data point.		Sandra J. Willisactingsrc
Deputy Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS)
U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
Nominated by Trump on 2026-06-25; not yet Senate-confirmed. Office vacant since predecessor Jim O'Neill (Deputy Secretary June 2025-Feb 2026) departed ~2026-02-13 to lead NSF. Klomp currently serves as HHS chief counselor and head of Medicare.		Chris KlompRepublicannominatedsrc
Deputy Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS)
U.S. Department of Homeland Security
Sworn in as 9th Deputy Secretary on 2025-03-08; nomination to be Ambassador to El Salvador withdrawn 2026-04-13, remained Deputy Secretary. Serves under Secretary Markwayne Mullin (confirmed 2026-03-24).		Troy EdgarRepublican2025-03-08incumbentsrc
Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis (DHS I&A)
U.S. Department of Homeland Security, Office of Intelligence and Analysis
Political appointee; Senate-confirmed 2025-07-31. A proposed FY27 reorg would fold I&A into the DHS Secretary's office while keeping IC membership.		Matthew KozmaRepublican2025-07-31incumbentsrc
Associate Attorney General (DOJ)
U.S. Department of Justice
Confirmed Oct 2025; assumed office 2025-11-14. April 5, 2026 reports of a possible Dhillon promotion / Woodward demotion remain unconfirmed.		Stanley Woodward Jr.Republican2025-11-14incumbentsrc
Deputy Attorney General (DOJ)
U.S. Department of Justice
Confirmed/sworn DAG March 2025 and still nominally holds the title (no successor DAG named). Became Acting Attorney General on 2026-04-02 after Trump fired Pam Bondi; nominated for permanent AG in June 2026.		Todd BlancheRepublican2025-03-06incumbentsrc
Solicitor General (DOJ)
U.S. Department of Justice
Confirmed by the Senate 52-45 on 2025-04-04, took office same day.		D. John SauerRepublican2025-04-04incumbentsrc
Deputy Secretary of State
U.S. Department of State
Confirmed by the Senate 2025-03-24; took office 2025-03-25. Principal Deputy Secretary (the No. 2 official at State).		Christopher LandauRepublican2025-03-25incumbentsrc
Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources
U.S. Department of State
Second deputy secretary position. SFRC advanced nomination 16-6 on 2025-03-12; assumed office 2025-05-19.		Michael J. RigasRepublican2025-05-19incumbentsrc
U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations
U.S. Department of State
32nd U.S. Permanent Representative to the UN. Confirmed 47-43 on 2025-09-19; sworn in 2025-09-20. Previously Trump's National Security Advisor.		Mike WaltzRepublican2025-09-20incumbentsrc
Acting Assistant Secretary of State for Intelligence and Research (INR)
U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Intelligence and Research
Career FSO serving in acting capacity since ~early 2025 (confirmed slot vacant after Brett Holmgren departed). CAVEAT: acting-holder identity rests on a single (Wikipedia) source and may be dated. Nominee Michael J. Vance placed on the Senate Executive Calendar 2026-06-23; not yet floor-confirmed.		Amb. Donald Blomeactingsrc
Deputy Secretary of Veterans Affairs
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
11th Deputy Secretary of VA; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-27, sworn in 2025-03-28 by Secretary Doug Collins. As of early 2026 also performing the delegable duties of Assistant Secretary for Information and Technology / CIO.		Paul R. LawrenceRepublican2025-03-28incumbentsrc
Deputy Secretary of the Treasury (Treasury)
U.S. Department of the Treasury
Michael Faulkender (16th Deputy Secretary, confirmed 2025-03-26) resigned 2025-08-22. Treasury announced (SB-0479, 2026-04-29) Brooke would perform the delegable duties of Deputy Secretary; also nominated to the post 2026-05-11 (pending). Concurrently Acting Under Secretary for International Affairs.		Francis BrookeRepublican2026-04-29actingsrc
Treasurer of the United States (Treasury)
U.S. Department of the Treasury
46th Treasurer; former Georgia (Republican) state senator; named by Trump 2025-03-23. Position no longer requires Senate confirmation. Start date varies by source.		Brandon BeachRepublican2025-05-28incumbentsrc
Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence (Treasury)
U.S. Department of the Treasury
Confirmed by the Senate 51-47 in July 2025 (Treasury announcement dated 2025-07-23), succeeding acting head Brad Smith.		John K. HurleyRepublican2025-07-23incumbentsrc

U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Intelligence and Analysis

U.S. Forest Service

Independent (32)
OfficePersonPartySinceStatusSource
Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Contested leadership. Russell Vought (also OMB Director) has been CFPB Acting Director since Feb. 2025 and remained acting as of late June 2026, but federal law bars him from serving past 2026-08-01. Per a 2026-06-02 report, Chief Legal Officer Mark Paoletta was elevated to Deputy Director and is slated to become Acting Director on Vought's exit. Separately, on 2026-06-10 President Trump nominated Brian Johnson as permanent CFPB Director (his third nominee, after Jonathan McKernan and Stuart Levenbach).		Russell VoughtRepublicanactingsrc
Acting Chairman of the Consumer Product Safety Commission
Consumer Product Safety Commission
Feldman (Vice Chairman, elected 2025-01-13) became Acting Chairman on 2025-01-21 when Alex Hoehn-Saric left the chairmanship. Trump removed the three Democratic commissioners (Hoehn-Saric, Mary Boyle, Richard Trumka Jr.) in 2025; the firings were upheld by the U.S. Supreme Court (Trump v. Boyle, July 2025). Still styled 'Acting Chairman' in 2026.		Peter A. FeldmanRepublican2025-01-21actingsrc
Chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
Lucas (Commissioner since 2020) was named Acting Chair on 2025-01-20, reconfirmed by the Senate 2025-07-31 for a term to 2030-07-01, and formally designated Chair (announced 2025-11-06).		Andrea R. LucasRepublican2025-11-06incumbentsrc
President and Chairman of the Export-Import Bank of the United States
Export-Import Bank of the United States
Confirmed by the U.S. Senate as President and Chairman (announced 2025-09-19); term expiring 2029-01-20.		John JovanovicRepublican2025-09-19incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Federal Communications Commission
Federal Communications Commission		Brendan CarrRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
Confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 23rd FDIC Chairman on 2026-01-13 for a five-year term. Had served as Acting Chairman from 2025-01-20 until confirmation.		Travis HillRepublican2026-01-13incumbentsrc
Chair of the Federal Election Commission
Federal Election Commission
FEC chair rotates annually. Broussard (Trump-nominated 2020) was elected Chair effective 2025-07-01 (Campaigns & Elections), her second time as chair (also 2021), succeeding Ellen Weintraub whom Trump moved to remove in Feb 2025. The FEC currently has only 2 of 6 commissioners (Broussard and Dara Lindenbaum) and lacks a 4-member policymaking quorum.		Shana M. BroussardDemocratic2025-07-01incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
Confirmed as Commissioner 2025-10-07 (term to 2030-06-30); designated Chairman 2025-10-23, succeeding acting chair David Rosner. Republican Mark Christie chaired FERC earlier in 2025 before departing.		Laura V. SwettRepublican2025-10-23incumbentsrc
Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency
Federal Housing Finance Agency
Senate-confirmed (March 2025); sworn in 2025-03-14 as 5th FHFA Director. In June 2026 Pulte was also named Acting Director of National Intelligence while retaining his FHFA directorship and chairmanship of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.		William J. PulteRepublican2025-03-14incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Federal Maritime Commission
Federal Maritime Commission
Trump designated DiBella as Chairman on 2026-01-28. Predecessor Louis E. Sola, designated chairman 2025-01-20, ended his tenure as Chairman on 2025-06-30.		Laura DiBellaRepublican2026-01-28incumbentsrc
Chair of the Federal Reserve
Federal Reserve System
Transition: Jerome H. Powell's term as Chair ended; the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh 54-45 on 2026-05-13 (NPR/CNBC) and he took the oath as Chair on 2026-05-22 (federalreserve.gov). Powell remains on the Board as a governor. Warsh is the 17th Fed Chair; four-year term runs to 2030.		Kevin WarshRepublican2026-05-22incumbentsrc
Vice Chair for Supervision of the Federal Reserve
Federal Reserve System		Michelle W. BowmanRepublican2025-06-09incumbentsrc
Vice Chair of the Federal Reserve
Federal Reserve System
Took office as Vice Chair on 2023-09-13 for a four-year term ending 2027-09-07; still serving as of 2026-06-29.		Philip N. JeffersonDemocratic2023-09-13incumbentsrc
Chair of the Federal Trade Commission
Federal Trade Commission		Andrew N. FergusonRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Administrator of the General Services Administration
General Services Administration
Senate confirmed Forst 2025-12-18; sworn in as GSA Administrator 2025-12-24. Stephen Ehikian had served as acting administrator earlier in 2025.		Edward C. Forst2025-12-24incumbentsrc
Acting Chairman of the Merit Systems Protection Board
Merit Systems Protection Board
Kerner (Vice Chairman, confirmed May 2024) became Acting Chairman in February 2025 after Trump removed Chair Cathy Harris; the removal was litigated (reinstated, then re-removed). The Senate confirmed James J. Woodruff II in October 2025, restoring the Board's quorum.		Henry J. KernerRepublicanactingsrc
Administrator of NASA
National Aeronautics and Space Administration
Trump nominated Isaacman in late 2024/early 2025; nomination withdrawn May 2025; re-nominated 2025-11-04. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy served as acting/interim administrator from July 2025 until Isaacman was confirmed by the Senate 67-30 on 2025-12-17 and sworn in (15th Administrator) 2025-12-18.		Jared Isaacman2025-12-18incumbentsrc
Chairman of the National Credit Union Administration
National Credit Union Administration
Designated NCUA Chairman by President Trump in January 2025. His six-year board term expired in August 2025, but he is serving in holdover capacity as Chairman pending a confirmed successor (John Crews nominated). Two NCUA board seats remain vacant amid litigation over the April 2025 dismissals of Todd Harper and Tanya Otsuka.		Kyle S. HauptmanRepublican2025-01-22incumbentsrc
Chairman of the National Labor Relations Board
National Labor Relations Board
After 2025 NLRB quorum/membership turmoil, Murphy was sworn in as a Board Member 2026-01-07 (term to 2027-12-16) and designated Chairman by President Trump on 2026-03-27, succeeding former Chairman Marvin Kaplan, to whom Murphy had been Chief Counsel.		James R. MurphyRepublican2026-03-27incumbentsrc
Acting Director of the National Science Foundation
National Science Foundation
Director Sethuraman Panchanathan resigned effective 2025-04-24; chief of staff Brian Stone has performed the duties of director since. President Trump nominated Jim (James) O'Neill as NSF Director on 2026-03-02; as of late June 2026 the nomination was still pending before the Senate HELP Committee, so Stone remains acting director.		Brian Stone2025-04-25actingsrc
Chair of the National Transportation Safety Board
National Transportation Safety Board
15th NTSB Chair since August 2021. The Senate reconfirmed her in 2024 for another five-year Board term and a three-year term as Chair; she continues as Chair in 2026.		Jennifer HomendyDemocratic2021-08-13incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
Nuclear Regulatory Commission
Nieh sworn in as NRC Commissioner 2025-12-04 (term ending 2029-06-30) and designated 20th Chairman by President Trump on 2026-01-08, replacing David Wright (designated chair 2025-01-21, remains a commissioner).		Ho K. Nieh2026-01-08incumbentsrc
Director of the Office of Personnel Management
Office of Personnel Management
Senate confirmed Kupor 49-46 on 2025-07-09; sworn in as OPM Director 2025-07-14.		Scott Kupor2025-07-14incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
Appointed by the SEC (announced 2026-01-30) and sworn in 2026-02-10 amid the 2025-2026 PCAOB leadership overhaul; term expires 2030-10-24. Sworn in alongside new board members Mark Calabria and Steven Laughton.		Demetrios Logothetis2026-02-10incumbentsrc
President and CEO of the Securities Investor Protection Corporation
Securities Investor Protection Corporation
Post became President and CEO on 2025-07-01, succeeding Josephine Wang, who retired 2025-06-30 after 41 years.		Michael L. Post2025-07-01incumbentsrc
Administrator of the Small Business Administration
Small Business Administration
28th SBA Administrator; former U.S. Senator from Georgia. Confirmed by the Senate 2025-02-19.		Kelly LoefflerRepublican2025-02-19incumbentsrc
Commissioner of Social Security
Social Security Administration
Senate confirmed Bisignano 53-47 on 2025-05-06; sworn in as the 18th Senate-confirmed SSA Commissioner in May 2025 (SSA marked his one-year anniversary on 2026-05-07).		Frank Bisignano2025-05-07incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Surface Transportation Board
Surface Transportation Board
Trump designated Fuchs as Chairman effective 2025-01-20; reconfirmed to the Board in 2024 with term to 2029-01-14. Continues as Chairman in 2026.		Patrick J. FuchsRepublican2025-01-20incumbentsrc
Chair of the U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
Designated Chair by unanimous vote of the commissioners in July 2023; commissioner term ends December 2028. Vice Chair is Victoria F. Nourse. Confirmed still Chair on the live USCCR commissioners page in 2026.		Rochelle M. GarzaDemocraticincumbentsrc
Chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission
Nominated 2025-10-27; confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 16th CFTC Chairman on 2025-12-22. Previously chief counsel of the SEC's Crypto Task Force. Caroline Pham had been acting chair earlier in 2025.		Michael S. SeligRepublican2025-12-22incumbentsrc
Chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission		Paul S. AtkinsRepublican2025-04-21incumbentsrc
Postmaster General and Chief Executive Officer, United States Postal Service
United States Postal Service
76th Postmaster General. Appointed by the USPS Board of Governors on 2025-05-09; took office 2025-07-15, succeeding Louis DeJoy (resigned March 2025) and acting PMG Doug Tulino.		David Steiner2025-07-15incumbentsrc
Military (28)
OfficePersonPartySinceStatusSource
Director, Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA)
Defense Intelligence Agency
U.S. Marine Corps. 25th DIA director; assumed 2026-02-20, succeeding Lt. Gen. Jeffrey Kruse (USAF).		Lt. Gen. James H. Adams III2026-02-20incumbentsrc
Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
Joint Chiefs of Staff
21st CJCS; recalled from retirement after Trump fired Gen. C.Q. Brown Jr. in Feb 2025. Sworn in 2025-04-11.		Gen. Dan Caine2025-04-11incumbentsrc
Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
Joint Chiefs of Staff
13th VCJCS (USMC); confirmed 2025-09-19, sworn in 2025-10-01, succeeding Adm. Christopher Grady.		Gen. Christopher J. Mahoney2025-10-01incumbentsrc
Director, National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA)
National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency
U.S. Army. 9th NGA director; assumed 2025-11-05, succeeding Vice Adm. Frank Whitworth (USN, retired).		Lt. Gen. Michele H. Bredenkamp2025-11-05incumbentsrc
Director, National Security Agency (NSA) — dual-hat CYBERCOM
National Security Agency
U.S. Army. Senate-confirmed 2026-03-10 (71-29); dual-hatted NSA Director + CYBERCOM commander (also listed as ccmd-cybercom).		Gen. Joshua M. Rudd2026-03-10incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM)
U.S. Africa Command
U.S. Air Force. Assumed command 2025-08-15 (Stuttgart), succeeding Gen. Michael Langley (USMC); Senate-confirmed by voice vote 2025-07-31. First USAF officer to lead AFRICOM.		Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson2025-08-15incumbentsrc
Chief of Staff of the Air Force
U.S. Air Force
24th CSAF; confirmed 2025-10-30, sworn in 2025-11-03, succeeding the retiring Gen. David Allvin.		Gen. Kenneth Wilsbach2025-11-03incumbentsrc
Deputy Chief of Staff for Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (A2)
U.S. Air Force
U.S. Air Force. Assumed the intelligence-only A2 role ~2025-08 after the combined A2/6 was split (start day approximate).		Lt. Gen. Max E. Pearson2025-08-12incumbentsrc
Acting Chief of Staff of the Army
U.S. Army
Vice Chief of Staff of the Army (40th, since 2026-02-06) became acting CSA after SecDef Hegseth removed Gen. Randy George (41st CSA) on 2026-04-02. No Senate-confirmed permanent successor reported as of June 2026.		Gen. Christopher LaNeve2026-04-02actingsrc
Deputy Chief of Staff, G-2 (Army Intelligence)
U.S. Army
U.S. Army. Promoted to lieutenant general 2025-12-05 into the G-2 role; previously CG, 7th Infantry Division (to Oct 2025); succeeded Lt. Gen. Anthony Hale. Exact change-of-charge date approximate.		Lt. Gen. Michelle A. Schmidt2025-12-05incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM)
U.S. Central Command
U.S. Navy. Assumed command 2025-08-08, succeeding Gen. Michael 'Erik' Kurilla (USA); first Navy officer to lead CENTCOM since 2008.		Adm. Charles B. 'Brad' Cooper II2025-08-08incumbentsrc
Assistant Commandant for Intelligence (CG-2)
U.S. Coast Guard, Coast Guard Intelligence
U.S. Coast Guard. In role since August 2022 (start day approximate); still listed as CG-2 in 2026 sources.		Rear Adm. Rebecca Ore2022-08-01incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Cyber Command (CYBERCOM) & Director, NSA
U.S. Cyber Command
U.S. Army (career special operations). Senate-confirmed 2026-03-10 (71-29); dual-hatted CYBERCOM cdr + NSA director. Ended ~a year of acting leadership (Lt. Gen. William Hartman acting) after Gen. Timothy Haugh was removed April 2025.		Gen. Joshua M. Rudd2026-03-10incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. European Command (EUCOM) & Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR)
U.S. European Command / NATO Allied Command Operations
U.S. Air Force. Assumed EUCOM 2025-07-01 and SACEUR 2025-07-04, succeeding Gen. Christopher Cavoli (USA).		Gen. Alexus G. Grynkewich2025-07-01incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Indo-Pacific Command (INDOPACOM)
U.S. Indo-Pacific Command
U.S. Navy. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-05-03), succeeding Adm. John Aquilino. DoD administratively rebranded the command 'U.S. Pacific Command' 2026-06-16; statutory name and leadership unchanged.		Adm. Samuel J. Paparo Jr.2024-05-03incumbentsrc
Commandant of the Marine Corps
U.S. Marine Corps
39th Commandant of the Marine Corps, in office since 2023-09-22; still serving as of June 2026.		Gen. Eric M. Smith2023-09-22incumbentsrc
Director of Intelligence (DIRINT), HQMC
U.S. Marine Corps
U.S. Marine Corps. Assumed Director of Intelligence 2025-06-09 (promoted Maj. Gen. 2025-01-06).		Maj. Gen. Mark A. Cunningham2025-06-09incumbentsrc
Acting Director of Naval Intelligence (N2)
U.S. Navy
Civilian (retired Rear Adm.). Effective 2026-05-04 OPNAV N2N6 (DCNO for Information Warfare) was disestablished; Parode leads the new OPNAV Intelligence Division (N2) and is designated Acting Director, Naval Intelligence. Branch marked 'military' per category, but the current acting holder is a civilian.		Steve Parode2026-05-04actingsrc
Chief of Naval Operations
U.S. Navy
34th CNO, sworn in 2025-08-25; followed Adm. Lisa Franchetti's removal (Feb 2025) and Adm. James Kilby's acting tenure.		Adm. Daryl Caudle2025-08-25incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Northern Command (NORTHCOM) & NORAD
U.S. Northern Command / North American Aerospace Defense Command
U.S. Air Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-02-05), dual-hatted NORTHCOM + NORAD; succeeded Gen. Glen VanHerck.		Gen. Gregory M. Guillot2024-02-05incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Southern Command (SOUTHCOM)
U.S. Southern Command
U.S. Marine Corps. Senate-confirmed 2026-02-02; assumed command 2026-02-05. Succeeded acting cdr Lt. Gen. Evan Pettus (USAF) after Adm. Alvin Holsey retired early.		Gen. Francis L. Donovan2026-02-05incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Space Command (SPACECOM)
U.S. Space Command
U.S. Space Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-01-10). Gen. Michael Guetlein (who left for the Golden Dome program) was Vice Chief of Space Operations, not the SPACECOM commander; Whiting remains.		Gen. Stephen N. Whiting2024-01-10incumbentsrc
Chief of Space Operations
U.S. Space Force
2nd Chief of Space Operations, in office since 2022-11-02; still serving per April 2026 Space Symposium and official bio.		Gen. B. Chance Saltzman2022-11-02incumbentsrc
Deputy Chief of Space Operations for Intelligence (S2)
U.S. Space Force
U.S. Space Force. Listed as S2 in the 2026 USAF/USSF Almanac. CONFLICT: one source (Wikipedia) reports Sidari departed ~March 2026 with civilian SES John J. Deeney IV serving as acting S2 — unverified against the Almanac; flagged for reconciliation.		Maj. Gen. Brian D. Sidariincumbentsrc
Commander, Space Delta 7 (ISR)
U.S. Space Force, Space Delta 7
U.S. Space Force. Senior ISR field commander; took command of Space Delta 7 (ISR) on 2024-06-13.		Col. Phoenix L. Hauser2024-06-13incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Special Operations Command (SOCOM)
U.S. Special Operations Command
U.S. Navy (SEAL). Senate-confirmed ~2025-08-01; assumed command 2025-10-03, succeeding Gen. Bryan Fenton (USA). Former JSOC commander.		Adm. Frank M. 'Mitch' Bradley2025-10-03incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Strategic Command (STRATCOM)
U.S. Strategic Command
U.S. Navy. Assumed command 2025-12-05, succeeding Gen. Anthony J. Cotton (USAF); career submariner, previously STRATCOM deputy.		Adm. Richard A. 'Rich' Correll2025-12-05incumbentsrc
Commander, U.S. Transportation Command (TRANSCOM)
U.S. Transportation Command
U.S. Air Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-10-04), succeeding Gen. Jacqueline Van Ovost; 15th TRANSCOM commander.		Gen. Randall Reed2024-10-04incumbentsrc
Law enforcement (22)
OfficePersonPartySinceStatusSource
ATF Director (DOJ)
Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives
Career ATF agent; Senate-confirmed ~2026-04-29 and sworn in 2026-04-30; previously ATF Deputy Director since April 2025.		Robert Cekada2026-04-30incumbentsrc
DEA Administrator (DOJ)
Drug Enforcement Administration
Sworn in July 2025 after Senate confirmation; still serving (DEA statement dated 2026-06-25).		Terrance C. ColeRepublican2025-07-23incumbentsrc
FBI Director (DOJ)
Federal Bureau of Investigation
9th FBI Director; sworn in 2025-02-20; confirmed serving as of June 2026.		Kash PatelRepublican2025-02-20incumbentsrc
Director of the Bureau of Prisons (DOJ)
Federal Bureau of Prisons
Appointed by President Trump and sworn in 2025-04-21; former West Virginia corrections commissioner. BOP Director is not a Senate-confirmed position.		William K. Marshall III2025-04-21incumbentsrc
TSA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
Transportation Security Administration
Named Deputy Administrator April 2025; serving as Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator through at least mid-2026. Trump nominated David Cummins as permanent TSA Administrator (~2026-05-11), pending Senate confirmation.		Ha Nguyen McNeillRepublicanactingsrc
USCIS Director (DHS)
U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services
Senate-confirmed; began service as Director on 2025-07-15.		Joseph B. EdlowRepublican2025-07-15incumbentsrc
Commandant of the Coast Guard (DHS)
U.S. Coast Guard
Uniformed officer (Admiral). Served as Acting Commandant 2025-01-21 to 2026-01-15, then sworn in as the 28th Commandant on 2026-01-15.		Kevin E. Lunday2026-01-15incumbentsrc
CBP Commissioner (DHS)
U.S. Customs and Border Protection
U.S. Senate confirmed 2025-06-18; sworn in shortly after.		Rodney S. ScottRepublican2025-07-23incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorneys — coverage note
U.S. Department of Justice
All 93 U.S. Attorney districts exist; only the highest-profile offices are individually enumerated here. Many 2025-2026 U.S. Attorney posts are interim/acting (AG 120-day designations, court appointments under 28 U.S.C. 546(d), or First Assistants serving as acting), and several have been the subject of court disqualification fights.		(multiple)incumbentsrc
First Assistant U.S. Attorney performing U.S. Attorney duties, Central District of California
U.S. District Court, Central District of California
Former Republican California assemblyman. Served as acting U.S. Attorney from July 2025; a court ruled he was unlawfully serving as 'acting' without Senate confirmation and barred that title, so he now performs U.S. Attorney duties as First Assistant U.S. Attorney.		Bill EssayliRepublicanactingsrc
U.S. Attorney, District of Columbia
U.S. District Court, District of Columbia
Senate-confirmed 2025-08-02 (sworn interim 2025-05-28), succeeding interim Ed Martin. Former NY judge/DA and Fox News host.		Jeanine PirroRepublican2025-08-02incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, District of Massachusetts
U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
Career federal prosecutor (23 yrs). Appointed interim by the Acting AG 2025-01-20; subsequently continued (court-appointed) as U.S. Attorney.		Leah B. Foley2025-01-20incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, District of New Jersey
U.S. District Court, District of New Jersey
Career prosecutor (20+ yrs in the office); court-appointed (28 U.S.C. 546(d)) by Chief Judge Renee Bumb on 2026-03-23; appointment later ruled lawful. Replaced Alina Habba (resigned 2025-12-08 after courts disqualified her) and a subsequently disqualified interim trio.		Robert Frazer2026-03-23incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, Eastern District of New York
U.S. District Court, Eastern District of New York
AG-designated interim (sworn 2025-05-05); court-appointed by EDNY judges, sworn 2025-09-02. Senate nomination returned to the President 2026-01-03; continues via court appointment.		Joseph Nocella Jr.2025-09-02incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, Eastern District of Virginia
U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Virginia
Contested/vacant as of as_of date. Erik Siebert (interim) resigned Sept 2025; Lindsey Halligan (interim) ruled unlawfully appointed Nov 2025 and left Jan 2026; court-appointed James W. Hundley (28 U.S.C. 546(d), 2026-02-20) was fired by DOJ 2026-02-21. A fresh court vacancy announcement was posted June 2026; no undisputed current holder identified.		(vacant — contested)actingsrc
U.S. Attorney, Northern District of California
U.S. District Court, Northern District of California
Career prosecutor; sworn in May 2025 (interim/court-appointed). Continues to serve in 2026.		Craig H. Missakianincumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, Northern District of Illinois
U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
AG 120-day interim (2025-04-07); court-appointed by N.D. Ill. judges effective 2025-08-05. Faced 2026 controversy over 'Broadview Six' dismissals and grand-jury practices; some members of Congress called for his resignation.		Andrew S. Boutros2025-08-05incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, Southern District of Florida
U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida
Senate-confirmed 2025-08-02; sworn 2025-08-13. First U.S. Attorney Senate-confirmed in Trump's second term. Former state judge and Army reservist; succeeded interim Hayden O'Byrne.		Jason A. Reding QuiñonesRepublican2025-08-13incumbentsrc
U.S. Attorney, Southern District of New York
U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York
AG-designated interim April 2025; court-appointed by SDNY judges August 2025. Former SEC chairman. Nominated by Trump to be DNI on 2026-06-11; remains SDNY U.S. Attorney pending confirmation.		Jay ClaytonRepublicanincumbentsrc
ICE Director, Acting (DHS)
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
Acting director since ~2026-06-01, succeeding Todd Lyons (Mar 2025-May 2026). On 2026-06-27 Trump named Lance Schroyer as nominee for permanent ICE Director. ICE has had no Senate-confirmed director since 2017.		David VenturellaRepublican2026-06-01actingsrc
U.S. Marshals Service Director (DOJ)
U.S. Marshals Service
13th USMS Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-07-31, sworn in 2025-08-01. Career law-enforcement official.		Gadyaces S. Serralta2025-08-01incumbentsrc
Secret Service Director (DHS)
U.S. Secret Service
28th Director; the Secret Service Director is a presidential appointment that does not require Senate confirmation.		Sean M. CurranRepublican2025-01-22incumbentsrc

Alabama sheriffs — coverage note
Alabama
All 67 Alabama counties individually web-verified as of 2026-07-03. Every county has an elected sheriff. Mid-term gubernatorial appointees now sitting: Autauga, Baldwin, Butler, Calhoun, Jackson, Perry. Party split among the 67: 48 R, 18 D, 1 blank (Jackson, appointed). 0 unverified. 2026 primary/runoff winners do not take office until Jan 2027.		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Alaska sheriffs — coverage note
Alaska
Alaska has NO counties and NO county sheriffs. The state is organized into boroughs plus a single Unorganized Borough (with no borough-wide government), and neither boroughs nor the unorganized borough elect sheriffs. Statewide/rural law enforcement is provided by the Alaska State Troopers (Dept of Public Safety), who also serve civil process; local police departments cover incorporated cities; and Village Public Safety Officers (VPSOs) provide policing in remote Native villages. No elected county sheriffs exist.		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Arizona

Arkansas sheriffs — coverage note
Arkansas
75 of 75 counties returned, all individually web-verified against county sheriff sites, county government pages, the Arkansas Sheriffs' Association, and/or Ballotpedia. 2026 is a sheriff election year (4-year terms; winners seated 2027-01-01), so every record reflects the CURRENT officeholder as of 2026-07-03, not primary/general-election winners. Mid-term turnover captured: Hot Spring (Richard Tolleson, appointed 2025-03-11 after Finkbeiner resigned), Union (Charlie Phillips, appointed Nov 2025 after Ricky Roberts resigned), Sebastian (Mike Conger, interim from 2026-04-01 after Hobe Runion retired). Incumbents not returning in 2027 but still serving through 2026-12-31: Baxter (Montgomery), Boone (Roy Martin), Cleveland (Rodgers), Craighead (Boyd), Hempstead (Singleton), Howard (McJunkins), Lonoke (Staley), Saline (Wright), Scott (Shores), White (Phillip Miller), Yell (Gault). Party is filled only where explicitly evidenced (Republican or Democratic); left blank for confirmed Independents (Columbia — Leroy Martin II) and where partisan affiliation was not independently verified, though most Arkansas sheriffs are Republican. Colorado sheriffs — coverage note
Colorado
64 of 64 Colorado counties (62 partisan-elected + Denver appointed + Broomfield police-chief-as-sheriff). Party: 35 R, 12 D, 17 blank. SPECIAL: Denver + Broomfield have appointed (non-elected) sheriffs. GENUINELY UNAFFILIATED elected: Dolores, Hinsdale, Ouray. Mid-term appointees/turnover (party blank): Chaffee, Clear Creek, Costilla, Moffat, Jackson (after Poley recall), San Juan, San Miguel, Sedgwick. Party INFERRED (lower confidence): Baca, Kit Carson, Logan, Morgan. Disambiguation: Cheyenne CO = Buchanan; Yuma = Combs; Teller = Mikesell.		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Connecticut sheriffs — coverage note
Connecticut
Connecticut has NO county sheriffs. The elected county-sheriff system was ABOLISHED by a constitutional amendment approved by voters on Nov 7, 2000 (repealing Art. IV §25 of the state constitution), effective Nov 30, 2000, after corruption scandals. Connecticut's counties have no county government. Former sheriff functions were transferred: courthouse security and prisoner transport/custody went to the Judicial Branch as judicial marshals; service of civil process went to state marshals, overseen by the autonomous State Marshal Commission in the Judicial Department. No elected county sheriffs exist.		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Delaware sheriffs — coverage note
Delaware
Delaware has 3 counties (New Castle, Kent, Sussex), each with a partisan-elected sheriff (4-yr term). Unlike the standard model, Delaware sheriffs have LIMITED powers: civil process and court/judicial service and conducting sheriff's (foreclosure/tax) sales only. They do NOT have general law-enforcement arrest or police powers — 2012 legislation (HB 325) declared sheriffs and deputies are not police officers with arrest authority, upheld by the Delaware Supreme Court in 2013 (Christopher v. Sussex County) after a Sussex County dispute. Current officeholders: New Castle — N. Scott Phillips (D); Kent — Brian E. Lewis (D); Sussex — Robert T. Lee (R). Delaware sheriff elections are held in 2022/2026/2030 (DE Dept of Elections schedule); the most recent was Nov 2022 and the next is Nov 2026 (not 2024).		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Florida sheriffs — coverage note
Florida
All 67 counties enumerated from the FDLE Sheriff's Office Directory (dated Sept 2, 2025), used as the flsheriffs.org-aligned backbone; still current as of July 2026. ~12 counties web-verified against county SO sites, Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, and FL Governor's office (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, Orange, Duval/Jacksonville, Hillsborough, Leon, Alachua, Gadsden, Volusia, Monroe, Osceola). Party breakdown: 59 Republican, 6 Democratic (Broward-Tony, Palm Beach-Bradshaw, Orange-Mina, Leon-McNeil, Alachua-Scott, Gadsden-Young), 1 no-party/independent (Volusia-Chitwood), 1 gubernatorial appointee left unaffiliated (Osceola-Blackmon). Notable turnover: Miami-Dade seated its first elected sheriff since 1966 (Rosie Cordero-Stutz, R, Jan 7 2025) after a 2018 constitutional amendment; Alachua flipped to Democrat Chad Scott (Jan 2025); Osceola Sheriff Marcos Lopez (D) suspended and arrested June 2025 on racketeering charges, DeSantis appointed ex-FHP chief Christopher Blackmon (EO 25-121). Note: Duval sheriff (Jacksonville) is the consolidated JSO.		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Georgia sheriffs — coverage note
Georgia
159 sheriff records returned, covering all 159 Georgia counties — no counties omitted. All names sourced from the Georgia Sheriffs' Association 2026 Annual Directory (published Jan 2026); ~48 counties independently cross-verified against Ballotpedia/official/news sources with no name discrepancies. Party filled where documented (metro/urban counties Democratic; verified rural counties Republican); left blank where not independently verified rather than assumed. Decatur County's Wiley Griffin is an Independent (party left blank, noted). Towns County turnover: elected Sheriff Kenneth Henderson (R, re-elected 2024) is suspended by Gov. Kemp (executive orders Oct 2025 and June 29, 2026) and Anthony Coleman serves as interim/acting sheriff as of 2026-07-03; the elected incumbent is recorded here. Other recent turnovers reflected: Richmond (Brantley, Jan 2025), Houston (Moulton, Jan 2025, succeeding late Sheriff Cullen Talton), Dougherty (Hayes, Dec 2024), Chatham (Coleman, Jan 2025), Thomas (Watkins, Dec 2024). Hawaii sheriffs — coverage note
Hawaii
Hawaii has NO elected county sheriffs. Sheriff functions are performed by a STATEWIDE Sheriff Division, which since Jan 1, 2024 sits under the new Hawaii Department of Law Enforcement (DLE) — split off from the former Dept of Public Safety under Act 278 / HB 2171. The DLE's sheriff deputies provide security and law enforcement for state facilities, the airport, and courts. Hawaii's 4 counties (Honolulu, Hawaii, Maui, Kauai) each run their own county police departments led by an appointed chief; there is no county sheriff. Current DLE Director: Mike Lambert (nominated Jan 2025 by Gov. Green, confirmed 2025).		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Idaho sheriffs — coverage note
Idaho
All 44/44 counties returned via the Idaho Sheriffs' Association 'Meet the Sheriffs' directory. Web-verified a representative sample against county/official/news sources: Ada (Clifford), Canyon (Donahue), Kootenai (Norris), Twin Falls (Johnson), Bonneville (Hulse), Latah (Skiles), Nez Perce (Scrimsher), Blaine (Ballis), plus ISA officers Butte (Hansen, 2026 pres.), Bannock (Manu), Adams (Zollman). Turnover: the ISA directory still lists Shoshone as 'Vacant', but the current sheriff is Shawn Wehr, a mid-term appointee sworn in 2026-03-27 (after Lindsey resigned Oct 2025, Eddy appointed Nov 2025 then resigned Feb 2026). Genuinely new via Nov 2024 election, seated 2025-01-13: Twin Falls (Johnson) and Blaine (Ballis). Party filled (all Republican) only where directly evidenced: 11 verified Republican; remaining 33 left blank per evidence rule (Idaho sheriffs are partisan-elected 4-yr terms and the state is overwhelmingly Republican, but individual party affiliation was not independently confirmed for those). No Democratic-affiliated sheriff was confirmed. Most recent general election Nov 2024, seated Jan 2025.		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff
Idaho County, Idaho		Doug Ulmerincumbentsrc
Illinois sheriffs — coverage note
Illinois
All 102 IL counties covered (102 sheriffs + this note). Primary backbone: IL State Board of Elections 'County Officers' directory (published 2025-12-16), which lists every county sheriff with party (R/D/I); cross-verified ~35 counties (Cook + collar counties DuPage/Lake/Will/Kane/McHenry + Winnebago/Sangamon/Champaign/McLean/Rock Island/Peoria/Macon/Madison/St. Clair/Williamson/Coles/Jackson/Henry/Moultrie/Shelby/Lee/Piatt/Union and others) against county sheriff sites, county-gov pages and Ballotpedia — all matched. Party split: 80 Republican, 20 Democratic, 2 Independent (Calhoun—William Heffington; Jersey—Nick Manns; Independents left party-blank per Republican|Democratic enum). All sheriffs are partisan-elected to 4-year terms; most recent general Nov 2024, seated Dec 2024. Mid-term turnover captured: Sangamon Co.—Paula Crouch appointed 2024-09-18 after Sheriff Jack Campbell resigned following the Sonya Massey shooting (first female Sangamon sheriff); ISBE Dec-2025 directory still lists Campbell but Crouch is the current incumbent. Cook County: Tom Dart (D), in office since 2006. Note: the 2016 constitutional-amendment vote abolished the elected Cook County Recorder, NOT the sheriff—sheriffs remain elected in all 102 counties. Several long-term sheriffs (DuPage/Mendrick—running for governor; McLean/Lane; Bureau/Reed; Christian/Kettelkamp; Kendall/Baird) are not seeking re-election in 2026 but remain incumbents as of 2026-07-04.		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff-Coroner
Imperial County, California
Full title 'Sheriff/Coroner/Marshal'.		Fred Miramontes2023-01incumbentsrc
Sheriff
Independence County, Arkansas		Shawn StephensRepublicanincumbentsrc
Sheriff
Indian River County, Florida		Eric FlowersRepublicanincumbentsrc
Indiana sheriffs — coverage note
Indiana
All 92 counties enumerated from the official Indiana Sheriffs directory (in.gov/sheriffs machine-readable roster) as the backbone. Verified a representative sample against county sheriff sites/Ballotpedia/local news: Marion (Forestal, D), Lake (Martinez, D), Allen (Hershberger, R), St. Joseph (Grzegorek, D), Porter (Balon, R), Vanderburgh (Robinson, D), Tippecanoe (Goldsmith, D), Monroe (Marte, D), plus ~16 more. Two mid-term corrections applied where the roster was stale: Vigo — Derek Fell (D) appointed Aug 2024 after Sheriff John Plasse died in office July 24, 2024; Clinton — Brendon Bright (R) sworn in May 23, 2026 via GOP caucus after Sheriff Richard Kelly resigned on a guilty plea. Note Dubois — Tom Kleinhelter remains the elected sheriff despite relinquishing law-enforcement certification (elected constitutional office; faces pending charges). Indiana sheriffs are partisan-elected to 4-year terms with a strict two-term limit; the major term-limit turnover cohort is 2026 (many 2-term incumbents — Forestal/Marion, Quakenbush/Hamilton, Grzegorek/St. Joseph, Burgess/Johnson, Wilder/Warrick — are term-limited, with successors elected Nov 2026 and seated Jan 2027), so most roster names remain the sitting incumbents on 2026-07-04. Party evidenced for 24 counties (Republican=17, Democratic=7); left blank for 68 where not individually verified — Indiana skews Republican but party was not assumed. Counts: 92 counties + 1 coverage note = 93 objects.		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff
Inyo County, California		Stephanie J. Rennie2023-01incumbentsrc
Iowa sheriffs — coverage note
Iowa
99/99 counties covered. Enumeration backbone = ISSDA (Iowa State Sheriffs' & Deputies' Association) 2025 Sheriff Directory (issda.org, dated April 4 2025). Sample web-verified against county sites/Ballotpedia: Polk (Schneider), Linn (Gardner), Scott (Lane), Des Moines (Glendening), Black Hawk (Neff). Iowa sheriffs are partisan-elected to 4-yr terms; most recent general Nov 2024, seated Jan 2025. Two mid-term changes captured since the directory: Iowa County — Todd Sauerbrei replaced Robert Rotter (resigned Jun 2 2026 to become U.S. Marshal, N.D. Iowa); Sac County — Kathryn 'Katie' Stange appointed Sep 2025 (first woman sheriff in Iowa in 100+ years) after Ken McClure retired and interim appointee Jonathan Meyer resigned. Party filled only where evidenced: Polk=Democratic, Scott=Republican; the vast majority of Iowa sheriffs are Republican but individual affiliation left blank where not directly confirmed. Turnover: Black Hawk (Neff, seated Jan 1 2025) and Iowa/Sac (mid-term, above).		(coverage)incumbentsrc

Kansas sheriffs — coverage note
Kansas
105/105 counties covered — every Kansas county elects a partisan sheriff (4-yr terms; general Nov 2024, seated Jan 13, 2025; Wyandotte/KCK unified gov still elects one, last elected Nov 2025). Enumeration backbone: Kansas Sheriffs' Association members directory (sort_by=county), which lists all 105. Web-verified sample (6): Johnson=Byron Roberson (D, first Dem in 96 yrs, seated 2025-01-13), Sedgwick/Wichita=Jeff Easter (R), Shawnee/Topeka=Brian Hill (R), Wyandotte/KCK=Daniel Soptic (D, reelected Nov 2025), Reno=Darrian Campbell (R, seated Jan 2025), Douglas=Jay Armbrister (D, unopposed 2024) — all matched the KSA directory exactly. Party filled only where evidenced (4 named: 3 D urban — Johnson, Douglas, Wyandotte; plus Sedgwick R, Shawnee R, Reno R = 6 total); remaining 99 left blank pending per-county evidence. Statewide, the large majority of Kansas sheriffs are Republican; the Democratic sheriffs sit in the urban KC/Lawrence metro counties. No mid-term vacancy/appointment turnover identified in the verified set; undersheriff acts and Governor appoints per K.S.A. 19-804 if a vacancy occurs. Note two easily-confused names: Wichita County (Leoti; Sheriff Kristopher Casper) is distinct from the city of Wichita (in Sedgwick County).		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff
Kearny County, Kansas		Michael Fontenotincumbentsrc
Sheriff
Kendall County, Illinois		Dwight BairdRepublicanincumbentsrc
Sheriff
Kent County, Delaware
Delaware sheriffs have limited civil-process powers only (serve court process, conduct sheriff's/foreclosure sales); no general arrest or police powers after 2012 statute (HB 325) and the 2013 Delaware Supreme Court ruling in Christopher v. Sussex County. Partisan-elected, 4-yr term; Delaware sheriff elections fall in 2022/2026/2030, so last elected Nov 2022, term through Dec 31, 2026, next election Nov 2026 — not 2024.		Brian E. LewisDemocraticincumbentsrc

Missouri sheriffs — coverage note
Missouri
115 jurisdictions covered: 114 counties + City of St. Louis (independent city, own elected sheriff). Enumeration backbone: State of Missouri Data Portal County Sheriff's Offices dataset (data.mo.gov/pzip-wwk6), which contains all 114 counties + St. Louis City with current sheriff names. Web-verified sample against county sites/Ballotpedia/local news: Jackson=Darryl Forté (D, re-elected 2024), Boone=Dwayne Carey (D), Jefferson=David Marshak (R), Clay=Will Akin (R), St. Charles=Scott A. Lewis (R), Greene=Jim Arnott (R). St. Louis COUNTY and St. Louis CITY are DISTINCT: County sheriff (Scott Kiefer) is APPOINTED and limited to court support/civil process (law enforcement = St. Louis County Police); City of St. Louis sheriff is elected with civil/court-security duties (SLMPD separate). TURNOVER: City of St. Louis — elected sheriff Alfred Montgomery (D) removed Dec 23, 2025 via quo warranto; John Hayden Jr. interim since Oct 2025. Greene — Sheriff Jim Arnott nominated for U.S. Marshal (Judiciary Cmte approved May 2026); still sitting as of 2026-07-04. Party evidenced for 6 jurisdictions (5 R / 1 D shown; Jackson+Boone Democratic); Missouri sheriffs are partisan-elected to 4-year terms (last general Nov 2024, seated Jan 2025) and the vast majority statewide are Republican, but party is left blank where not individually verified. Party filled: Republican=4, Democratic=2, blank=109.		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff
Mitchell County, Georgia		W.E. BozemanRepublicanincumbentsrc
Sheriff
Mitchell County, Iowa		Gregory Beaverincumbentsrc
Sheriff

New Castle County, Delaware
Delaware sheriffs have limited civil-process powers only (serve court process, conduct sheriff's/foreclosure sales); no general arrest or police powers after 2012 statute (HB 325) and the 2013 Delaware Supreme Court ruling in Christopher v. Sussex County. Partisan-elected, 4-yr term; Delaware sheriff elections fall in 2022/2026/2030 (per DE Dept of Elections schedule), so last elected Nov 2022, term through Dec 31, 2026, next election Nov 2026 — not 2024. Rhode Island sheriffs — coverage note
Rhode Island
Rhode Island has NO elected county sheriffs. Sheriffs are STATE employees, APPOINTED (not elected), organized nominally by the 5 counties but centrally administered as the Division of Sheriffs within the RI Department of Public Safety (R.I. Gen. Laws §42-7.3-3.2). The division handles courthouse security, prisoner transport, and service of civil process statewide. It is led by a Chief Sheriff: Paul M. Martellini, sworn in Oct 16, 2024 (acting since April 2024). No elected county sheriffs exist.		(coverage)incumbentsrc
Sheriff
